Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a curvature-aware precision routing framework can efficiently optimize mixed-precision quantization for deep neural networks, leading to improved accuracy and reduced computational complexity. By leveraging geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies, we can develop a training-free routing protocol that replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT have explored mixed-precision quantization and adaptive precision routing for deep neural networks. However, these approaches rely on heuristic-based methods, manual configurations, or training-based search methods, which can be time-consuming and suboptimal. In contrast, our proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework integrates spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, bridging foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. This approach prioritizes mathematical transparency, explicit numerical stability analysis, and strict computational overhead bounds, establishing a highly feasible pipeline for exploratory single-GPU validation.
Abstract: This paper proposes a curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient mixed-precision quantization in deep neural networks. By leveraging geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies, we develop a training-free routing protocol that replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Our framework integrates spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, bridging foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through extensive experiments on various benchmark datasets, showcasing improved accuracy and reduced computational complexity compared to state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework on benchmark datasets such as ImageNet and CIFAR-10
Compare the performance of our approach with state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods, including EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT
Investigate the impact of different curvature-aware routing strategies on the accuracy and computational complexity of deep neural networks
Analyze the trade-offs between accuracy, computational complexity, and memory usage in our curvature-aware precision routing framework
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may require significant computational resources for large-scale deep neural networks
The curvature-aware routing protocol may not be effective for all types of deep neural networks or benchmark datasets
The framework may require careful tuning of hyperparameters to achieve optimal performance
